feedback.text: I recommend extending the scope of "unfair commercial practices" to cover significant, unfair post-purchase changes to user agreements. For example, Futurehome customers who purchased a "lifetime" service were later required either to start paying a subscription fee or to lose access to their home-automation system. Likewise, when a software publisher discontinues support and shuts down authentication or other servers, legitimate purchasers of licensed software or games are left unable to use the products they paid for. Defining what is "significant" or "unfair" may be complex, but in my opinion allowing providers to just "turn off" products that were paid for does not seem "fair".
